items.title: D. Rykner says the Louvre needs to be 'refurbished', Macron's big project is 'useless and expensive'
items.description: Stuart Norval is pleased to welcome Didier Rykner, Art Historian, Investigative Journalist and Founder / Editor in Chief of La Tribune de l’Art. Offering a forceful critique of the Louvre's priorities, Rykner argues that the museum's challenge is not a lack of resources, rather a misallocation of them. While acknowledging that security is a both necessary and inevitable expense, he contends that the greater threat to the Louvre lies in the deterioration of its historic fabric and the diversion of funding towards what he regards as an unnecessary, politically driven architectural expansion.

items.title: Louvre Museum's Apollo gallery reopens, almost a year after the heist
items.description: It is the gallery from where 88 million euros worth of France's crown jewels were stolen last year in a dramatic heist. No jewels will be on display in the room – it will revert to its 17th century function as a state gallery. Details by FRANCE 24 journalist Carys Garland.

items.title: French mayor secreted away under threat by notorious DZ mafia drug gang
items.description: Here in France, authorities are waging an increasingly intense battle against rising drug crime. That fight took a new turn this week – as the mayor of the southern town of Alès was spirited from his home by police in the middle of the night over fears for his safety. He'd received multiple threats from the drug gang known as the “DZ Mafia”. Once a local player on the Marseille drug market, the gang has expanded to become one of France's top security threats. Details by Bryan Quinn and our colleagues at France Televisions.

items.description: A Ghanaian High Court has sentenced Bernard Antwi Boasiako, popularly known as Chairman Wontumi, to 20 years in prison with hard labour for illegal mining.

items.title: Gaborone accuses Moscow of luring Batswana to battlefield
items.description: Botswana is the latest nation to accuse Moscow of using deceptive tactics to lure foreign recruits to the battlefield. Without saying how many of its citizens were involved, Gaborone's foreign ministry claimed its nationals were duped into going to Russia and then forced into active combat.

items.title: Skiing enthusiasts flock to slopes on Lesotho's Maluti Mountain
items.description: Afriski says about 70 per cent of visitors come from neighbouring South Africa while the rest are mostly from across the region.

items.title: 🔴 Daniel Siad, French modelling scout linked to US sex offender Epstein, found dead outside Paris
Cameroon: President Paul Biya’s prolonged absence raises questions
items.description: Described as one of the longest-serving presidents in Africa, Cameroonian President Paul Biya's absence from the country continues to draw criticism.

items.title: Stories that caught our eye: July 10 to 17
items.description: A selection of stories that caught our eye over the last seven days in Argentina. Leer más

items.title: US military continues strikes on Iran as attacks overshadow diplomacy
items.description: The U.S. military said it completed the 11th night of strikes on Iran early Wednesday as attacks across the Middle East overshadowed diplomatic efforts in Pakistan to salvage an interim ceasefire deal that has collapsed.

items.title: Plas Johnson, ‘Pink Panther’ saxophonist, Grammy winner, dies at 94
items.description: Johnson specialised in jump-blues and R&B before becoming a well-respected live and studio session player who backed many greats

items.title: Meloni orders full probe after arrest death sparks unrest
items.description: Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni has called for a full investigation into the death of a man during an arrest in Bologna, while strongly condemning violent clashes that left dozens of police officers injured.

items.title: UK's Bloomsbury among beneficiaries of $1.5 billion Anthropic copyright lawsuit settlement
Finally Finalissima
items.description: Not even the full fury of Middle East conflict could avert the Finalissima between Argentina and Spain… Leer más

items.title: Africa CDC urges U.S. to lift Uganda Ebola travel restrictions
items.description: Africa's top public health agency is urging the United States to lift travel restrictions on Uganda, arguing the country has brought its Ebola outbreak under control.

items.title: Judge hearing evidence against singer D4vd in killing, dismemberment of teen
items.description: Prosecutors on Tuesday showed a body camera video with singer D4vd telling sheriff's deputies who were investigating a missing person's report that he had only met then-13-year-old Celeste Rivas Hernandez once in person, and he did not know she was a minor.

items.title: Watch: Flooded towns and evacuations as Chile hit by heavy rain
items.description: A "state of catastrophe" has been declared in Coquimbo and Huasco following the downpour.

items.title: Chile: Several dead, missing in severe rainstorm
items.description: President Jose Antonio Kast has declared a state of emergency in the affected areas of the country.

items.title: Why the youth jobs crisis is becoming global
items.description: Young people in much of the world face growing competition for entry-level jobs as graduate numbers rise, hiring slows and AI-driven disruption adds uncertainty to career prospects.

items.title: Senegal makes U-turn to back ex-president Sall for UN chief
items.description: In tonight's edition, a deadly ambush in northern Mali leaves at least 50 soldiers dead. Also, Nigeria is facing a new challenge in its fight against jihadist insurgents as foreign fighters join armed groups. And Senegalese President Bassirou Diomaye Faye is backing former president Macky Sall's bid to become UN Secretary-General.

items.title: Spain, Algeria seek fresh start as Sánchez visits Algiers
items.description: Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez says he wants to deepen ties with Algeria as the two countries rebuild relations following a diplomatic rift over Western Sahara.

items.title: Canada evaluating 'all options' after fresh US tariffs threat
items.description: Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney said Tuesday he was looking at "all options" after US President Donald Trump made plans to impose new 50 percent tariffs on many Canadian goods in 30 days. Carney also said that he and Trump had agreed to "intensify discussions" in the coming weeks on a possible deal, but did not suggest he would travel to Washington for any talks. FRANCE 24's Christopher Guly reports from Ottawa.

items.title: Spain shatters Argentina's dream with 1-0 win in World Cup final
items.description: Disappointment for Argentina and skipper Lionel Messi in World Cup final; Barcelona striker Ferran Torres lashes home a thumping finish in the 106th minute to reward a dominant display and win Spain the World Cup. Leer más

items.title: Ukraine hits more warehouses of top Russian online retailer
items.description: Ukrainian drones have hit two more warehouses belonging to Russia’s biggest online retailer Wildberries.

items.title: Details of allegations against Alan Jones emerge after judge decision
items.description: Details of allegations against veteran broadcaster Alan Jones can be revealed, after a non-publication order was lifted by a judge.

items.title: Germany news: Resistance to Nazi terror commemorated
items.description: Monday marks 82 years since "Operation Valkyrie," the failed attempt on Adolf Hitler's life in 1944. Meanwhile, Foreign Minister Wadephul is heading to Africa on official business.

items.title: Trump allows direct US flights to Lebanon in historic move
items.description: It comes over 40 years after the US suspended the direct route with Lebanon. The announcement was made after US President Donald Trump met with Lebanese President Joseph Aoun.

items.title: $16 million mansion set for demolition catches fire twice in a night
items.description: Fire broke out twice in one night at a $16-million mansion on the NSW Mid North Coast. It was approved for demolition just weeks ago, and police are treating the blazes as suspicious.

items.title: Taking stock of 2026 World Cup: Football's biggest show yet
items.description: A worthy world champion, a call from the US president, security concerns and a few pleasant surprises: The biggest World Cup ever is now over. So what will we remember about the 2026 World Cup?

items.title: Turkey-PKK talks: Is peace finally possible?
items.description: It was a year ago that PKK fighters held a symbolic disarmament ceremony, burning their weapons. Peace talks between the PKK and Turkey are going slowly, but some observers believe that they will succeed this time.

items.title: Gabon signs initial agreement on manganese during state visit to France
items.description: The President of Gabon's state visit to France got off to a strong start on Monday with the signing of three strategic agreements. French President Macron welcomed Brice Oligui Nguema to the Elysee Palace for discussions focused on strengthening political and economic cooperation.

items.title: US expands strikes on 11th straight night of attacks
items.description: The US military’s 11th consecutive night of strikes extended to Iranian provinces not previously hit.

items.title: Hiding behind the World Cup, Milei siblings restructure their government
items.description: The World Cup provided the Milei administration a much-needed breather after three months under siege. Javier and Karina Milei are learning by doing, but by doing so they are slowly but surely resembling what they initially promised to battle. Leer más

items.title: Milei planning trips to Brazil, Peru, Ecuador, Colombia – and UK?
items.description: Argentina’s leader is preparing for a fresh round of regional diplomacy, including a visit to see former Brazilian president Jair Bolsonaro, a trip to Peru for the inauguration of Keiko Fujimori, and bilateral meetings with Ecuador's Daniel Noboa and Colombia's president-elect Abelardo de la Espriella. Oh, and then there’s the issue of a trip to London… Leer más

items.title: Why is Trump targeting Iran’s Pickaxe Mountain nuclear site? Here’s what we know
items.description: U.S. President Donald Trump has escalated tensions with Iran by threatening to bomb the under-construction Pickaxe Mountain nuclear site, located south of Tehran. Why is he targeting it?

items.title: Messi on eve of final: Argentina has already written history 'no-one can erase'
items.description: Argentina captain says the team's greatest achievement has been the journey rather than the trophies as he delivers an emotional message to fans before Sunday's World Cup final. Leer más

items.title: Austria turns Adolf Hitler’s birthplace into police station
items.description: Austrian politicians hope the change will prevent neo-Nazis from visiting the building as a place of pilgrimage.

items.title: 'Works every time': Superstition ramps up ahead in Argentina of World Cup final
items.description: Football fans are putting their faith in good luck rituals ahead of Sunday's World Cup final. But these wacky habits are no joke to those who practice them. Leer más

items.title: Andy Burnham becomes Britain's new Prime Minister
items.description: Andy Burnham is Britain's new prime minister. After meeting King Charles III at Buckingham Palace, Burnham officially took over from Keir Starmer, who stepped down after just two years in office.
